message: Node started: Identify the smallest prime p such that there exists an integer n satisfying n^4 + 1 is divisible by p^2. To do this, first check primes p where the congruence n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p. For each such p, check if any root n of n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p^2 by testing if n^4 + 1 is divisible by p^2. Provide the value of p and the smallest positive integer n that satisfies n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p^2.
